title: Emoji Madness Takes Over the World!
story: Every July 17, millions of people celebrate World Emoji Day by flooding social media with their favorite tiny pictures! This fun holiday started because the calendar emoji on iPhones shows July 17. People post emoji-only messages, create emoji art, and try to tell entire stories using just smiley faces, hearts, and pizza slices. Companies release new emojis, and friends challenge each other to emoji puzzles. Some people even dress up as their favorite emoji characters! It's amazing how these little symbols help us share feelings and ideas across the world, no matter what language we speak.
activity_question: Why was July 17 chosen for World Emoji Day?
activity_choices: Because the calendar emoji on iPhones shows July 17
Because it's the day emojis were invented
Because it's when the first smiley face was drawn
Because July has 17 different emoji categories
activity_answer: Because the calendar emoji on iPhones shows July 17
follow_up_question: What story would you tell using only five emojis?

title: Peachy Keen Ice Cream Scene
story: Every July 17, ice cream lovers across America celebrate National Peach Ice Cream Day by scooping up this fruity frozen treat! Ice cream shops create special peach flavors using fresh summer peaches, while families make homemade versions in their kitchens. Some people host peach ice cream parties where everyone brings different peach desserts to share. Social media fills with photos of peachy scoops and creative sundaes. Many ice cream parlors offer discounts on peach flavors, and some even hold peach ice cream eating contests. It's the perfect way to enjoy sweet, juicy peaches on a hot summer day!
activity_question: What do ice cream shops use to make special peach ice cream flavors?
bonus_fact: Peach ice cream was one of George Washington's favorite desserts!

title: National Yellow Pig Day Goes Hog Wild
story: Every July 17, math lovers celebrate National Yellow Pig Day by honoring the number 17 and imaginary yellow pigs! This quirky holiday started when two math students got obsessed with the prime number 17 and decided it deserved its own mascot. Today, people draw yellow pigs, solve math puzzles involving 17, and share pig jokes online. Some schools host yellow pig parties where kids wear yellow and learn fun facts about the number 17, like how it appears in nature and math patterns.
activity_question: What number do people celebrate on National Yellow Pig Day?
follow_up_question: What other numbers do you think deserve their own animal mascot?

title: Wrong Way Corrigan's Crazy Flight Mix-Up
story: Douglas Corrigan became famous for making the most epic navigation mistake in aviation history! On July 17, people celebrate Wrong Way Corrigan Day by remembering his incredible blunder. In 1938, Corrigan was supposed to fly from New York to California, but somehow ended up in Ireland instead - claiming he followed the wrong end of his compass needle! Today, people mark this hilarious holiday by sharing their own funny mistakes, posting "wrong way" photos on social media, and celebrating the idea that sometimes our biggest goofs become our greatest adventures. Some folks even wear their clothes backwards or take detours on purpose to honor Corrigan's legendary mix-up!
activity_question: What mistake did Douglas Corrigan make that led to this holiday?
activity_choices: He flew to Ireland instead of California
He landed his plane upside down
He forgot to bring a map
He flew backwards the entire trip
activity_answer: He flew to Ireland instead of California
bonus_fact: Corrigan's plane was so old and beat-up that people called it a "flying crate"!
